revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:小室浅間神社 (富士吉田市大明見)]] {{distinguish|{{ill|Ōmuroyama Sengen Shrine|ja|大室山浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuroyama Sengen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|{{ill|Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)|ja|小室浅間神社_(富士吉田市大明見)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)|qq=}}|{{ill|Fuji Omuro Asama Shrine|ja|冨士御室浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Omuro Asama Shrine|qq=}}}} {{Unreferenced|date=September 2019}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Komuro Sengen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:北東本宮小室浅間神社 HokutouHonguOmuroSengenJinjya.jpg|thumb|Worship Hall]] |ISO = JP-19 |location = Main Shrine: {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida|1=Fujiyoshida}}, Daimogumi 2-1-1
Former Shrine: Yamanashi Prefecture, Fujiyoshida, Daimogumi 1-36 |coordinates = Main Shrine: {{coordinates|35|29|03.0|N|138|49|08.5|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Komuro Sengen Shrine - Main Shrine}}
Former Shrine: {{coordinates|35|29|05.6|N|138|49|47.5|E|region:JP-19_type:landmark|name=Komuro Sengen Shrine - Former Shrine}} |Deities = {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} |Shrine Body = |type = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} ({{ill|Shrines receiving offerings|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}) |consecration_year = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} 6th year (92 BCE) |Main Hall = Single-bay Irimoya-zukuri style with Karahafu eaves |alternate_name = Daimogumi Komuro Sengen Shrine
Northeastern Main Shrine Komuro Sengen Shrine
Northeastern Main Shrine of Mount Fuji Komuro Sengen Shrine |festival = April 15 |Rituals = Daimogumi Grand {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} }} [[File:Hokutouhongu_omurosengenjinja.jpg|alt=File:Hokutouhongu omurosengenjinja.jpg|thumb|250x250px|The main hall of the Hokutō Hongū Komuro Sengen Shrine (photographed in 2020). It is constructed in the 'Ikken-sha' style, featuring an 'irimoya' (hip-and-gable) roof with a 'karahafu' (undulating gable) on the eaves.]] '''Omuro Sengen Shrine''' (小室浅間神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida|1=Fujiyoshida}}, Daimogumi. It is one of the {{ill|Sengen shrine|en|Sengen shrine|qid=Q3471785|lt=Asama shrines|1=Sengen shrine}}. Its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} ({{ill|Shrines receiving offerings|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrines receiving offerings|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}). == History == The shrine's origins date back to the 6th year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} (92 BCE) with the enshrinement of the Asotani Shrine. Later, to calm the eruptions of {{ill|Mount Fuji|en|Mount Fuji|qid=Q39231|lt=Mount Fuji|1=Mount Fuji}}, the second prince of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} became the shrine guardian, renaming it Asotani Miyamori Shrine. During the 2nd year of {{ill|Emperor Sushun|en|Emperor Sushun|qid=Q349238|lt=Emperor Sushun|1=Emperor Sushun}}'s reign (589 CE), it was renamed Fuji Yamamoto Asama Myojin by {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}}. In {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|qid=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} 3 (1686 CE), the shrine was relocated from Daimogumi Village's Koyashiki area (present-day Fujiyoshida, Daimogumi 1-chome) to its current location in Shinyashiki (present-day Fujiyoshida, Daimogumi 2-chome). A shrine previously enshrined there, Fukuchi Hachiman Daijingu, was integrated into the new site. The old shrine (Former Shrine) still houses a stone shrine. The shrine was revered by {{ill|Daimyo|en|Daimyo|qid=Q61982|lt=daimyo|1=Daimyo}} and local lords. In {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 9 (1669), Akimoto Tajima-no-kami of Tanimura Castle made offerings, followed by Matsudaira Kai-no-kami in 1706. During the Meiji period, the shrine was designated a village shrine in 1872 and as a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1909. The shrine was renamed Northeastern Main Shrine Komuro Sengen Shrine in 1886. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Uhijini and Suhijini|ja|ウヒヂニ・スヒヂニ|lt=Uhijini-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uhijini and Suhijini|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Uwatsutsuno-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} == Sub-shrines == * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} (Daimogumi Gokoku Shrine) == Cultural Properties == * Daimogumi Sengen Shrine's Konara Oak (designated as a Fujiyoshida City Natural Monument on February 1, 1994)[https://web.fujinet.ed.jp/div/hakubutsu/pdf/bunkazaiichiran20190901.pdf List of Cultural Properties of Fujiyoshida City], Fujiyoshida City Board of Education, September 20, 2019.[https://web.fujinet.ed.jp/forms/info/info.aspx?info_id=629 City Designated Natural Monuments], Fujiyoshida City Board of Education. == location == Main Shrine: {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujiyoshida|en|Fujiyoshida|qid=Q647508|lt=Fujiyoshida|1=Fujiyoshida}}, Daimogumi 2-1-1 Former Shrine: Yamanashi Prefecture, Fujiyoshida, Daimogumi 1-36 == Miyashita Documents == The ancient records and manuscripts inherited by the Miyashita family, who serve as the shrine priests, are collectively known as the {{ill|Miyashita Documents|ja|宮下文書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyashita Documents|1=Q17217621|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17217621}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|ja|冨士御室浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha|qq=}} * {{ill|Omuro Sengen Shrine (Yamanashi)|lt=Omuro Sengen Shrine (Yamanashi)|lt=Fuji Yamamoto Shrine|ja|冨士山下宮小室浅間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Yamamoto Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamanashi-jinjacho.or.jp/intro/search/detail/7021 Komuro Sengen Shrine], Yamanashi Prefecture Shrine Association {{Pref-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|小室浅間神社 (富士吉田市大明見)|version=101938308|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小室浅間神社]] {{Commons category|Hokutō-hongū Omuro-sengen-jinja}} [[ja:小室浅間神社 (富士吉田市大明見)]] {{wikidata link|Q85879648}} [[Category:Asama shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Fujiyoshida]]
